name: read_bcf
signature: read_bcf(path, region := NULL, index_path := NULL, tidy_format := FALSE)
description: Read VCF and BCF variant data with typed INFO, FORMAT, and optional tidy sample output.
examples: SELECT CHROM, POS, REF, ALT FROM read_bcf('vcf_file.bcf') LIMIT 5;

name: read_bam
signature: read_bam(path, standard_tags := FALSE, auxiliary_tags := FALSE, region := NULL, index_path := NULL, reference := NULL)
description: Read SAM, BAM, and CRAM alignments with optional typed SAMtags and auxiliary tag maps.
examples: SELECT QNAME, FLAG, RNAME, POS FROM read_bam('range.bam') LIMIT 5;

name: read_fasta
description: Read FASTA records or indexed FASTA regions as sequence rows.

name: read_bed
description: Read BED3-BED12 interval files with canonical typed columns and optional tabix-backed region filtering.
examples: SELECT chrom, start, "end", name FROM read_bed('targets.bed') LIMIT 5;

name: fasta_nuc
signature: fasta_nuc(path, bed_path := NULL, bin_width := NULL, region := NULL, index_path := NULL, bed_index_path := NULL, include_seq := FALSE)
description: Compute bedtools nuc-style nucleotide composition for supplied BED intervals or generated fixed-width bins over a FASTA reference.
examples: SELECT chrom, start, "end", pct_gc FROM fasta_nuc('ce.fa', bin_width := 1000) LIMIT 5;

name: read_fastq
description: Read single-end, paired-end, or interleaved FASTQ files.
examples: SELECT NAME, MATE FROM read_fastq('r1.fq', mate_path := 'r2.fq') LIMIT 5;

name: read_gff
signature: read_gff(path, header_names := NULL, header := FALSE, column_types := NULL, auto_detect := FALSE, attributes_map := FALSE, region := NULL, index_path := NULL)
description: Read GFF annotations with optional parsed attribute maps and indexed region filtering.
examples: SELECT seqname, feature, start, "end" FROM read_gff('gff_file.gff.gz') LIMIT 5;

name: read_gtf
signature: read_gtf(path, header_names := NULL, header := FALSE, column_types := NULL, auto_detect := FALSE, attributes_map := FALSE, region := NULL, index_path := NULL)
description: Read GTF annotations with optional parsed attribute maps and indexed region filtering.
examples: SELECT seqname, feature, start, "end" FROM read_gtf('annotations.gtf.gz') LIMIT 5;

name: read_tabix
signature: read_tabix(path, header_names := NULL, header := FALSE, column_types := NULL, auto_detect := FALSE, attributes_map := FALSE, region := NULL, index_path := NULL)
description: Read generic tabix-indexed text data with optional header handling and type inference.

name: fasta_index
description: Build a FASTA index and return the index path used by the operation.

name: bgzip
signature: bgzip(path, output_path := NULL, threads := 4, level := -1, keep := TRUE, overwrite := FALSE)
description: Compress a plain file to BGZF and return the created output path and byte counts.

name: bgunzip
signature: bgunzip(path, output_path := NULL, threads := 4, keep := TRUE, overwrite := FALSE)
description: Decompress a BGZF-compressed file and return the created output path and byte counts.

name: bam_index
description: Build a BAM or CRAM index and report the written index path and format.

name: bcf_index
signature: bcf_index(path, index_path := NULL, min_shift := NULL, threads := 4)
description: Build a TBI or CSI index for a VCF or BCF file and report the written index path and format.

name: tabix_index
signature: tabix_index(path, preset := 'vcf', index_path := NULL, min_shift := 0, threads := 4, seq_col := NULL, start_col := NULL, end_col := NULL, comment_char := NULL, skip_lines := NULL)
description: Build a tabix index for a BGZF-compressed text file using a preset or explicit coordinate columns.

name: read_hts_header
description: Inspect HTS headers in parsed, raw, or combined form across supported formats.
examples: SELECT record_type, id FROM read_hts_header('formatcols.vcf.gz') LIMIT 10;

name: read_hts_index
description: Inspect high-level HTS index metadata such as sequence names and mapped counts.

name: read_hts_index_spans
description: Expand index metadata into span and chunk rows suitable for low-level index inspection.
examples: SELECT seqname, chunk_beg_vo, chunk_end_vo FROM read_hts_index_spans('vcf_file.bcf') LIMIT 5;

name: read_hts_index_raw
description: Return the raw on-disk HTS index blob together with basic identifying metadata.

name: seq_revcomp
description: Compute the reverse complement of a DNA sequence using A, C, G, T, and N bases.

name: seq_canonical
description: Return the lexicographically smaller of a sequence and its reverse complement.

name: seq_hash_2bit
description: Encode a short DNA sequence as a 2-bit unsigned integer hash.

name: seq_encode_4bit
description: Encode an IUPAC DNA sequence as a list of 4-bit base codes, preserving ambiguity symbols including N.

name: seq_decode_4bit
description: Decode a list of 4-bit IUPAC DNA base codes back into a sequence string.

name: seq_gc_content
description: Compute GC fraction for a DNA sequence as a value between 0 and 1.

name: seq_kmers
description: Expand a sequence into positional k-mers with optional canonicalization.

name: sam_flag_bits
description: Decode a SAM flag into a struct of boolean bit fields using explicit SAM-oriented names such as `is_paired`, `is_proper_pair`, `is_next_segment_unmapped`, and `is_supplementary`.

name: sam_flag_has
description: Test whether any bits from the provided SAM flag mask are set in a flag value.

name: is_forward_aligned
description: Test whether a mapped segment is aligned to the forward strand. Returns `NULL` for unmapped segments because SAM flag `0x10` does not define genomic strand when `0x4` is set.

name: cigar_has_soft_clip
description: Test whether a CIGAR string contains any soft-clipped segment (`S`).

name: cigar_has_hard_clip
description: Test whether a CIGAR string contains any hard-clipped segment (`H`).

name: cigar_left_soft_clip
description: Return the left-end soft-clipped length from a CIGAR string, or zero if the alignment does not start with `S`.

name: cigar_right_soft_clip
description: Return the right-end soft-clipped length from a CIGAR string, or zero if the alignment does not end with `S`.

name: cigar_query_length
description: Return the query-consuming length from a CIGAR string, counting `M`, `I`, `S`, `=`, and `X`.

name: cigar_aligned_query_length
description: Return the aligned query length from a CIGAR string, counting `M`, `=`, and `X` but excluding clips and insertions.

name: cigar_reference_length
description: Return the reference-consuming length from a CIGAR string, counting `M`, `D`, `N`, `=`, and `X`.

name: cigar_has_op
description: Test whether a CIGAR string contains at least one instance of the requested operator.

name: is_paired
description: Test whether the SAM flag indicates that the template has multiple segments in sequencing (`0x1`).

name: is_proper_pair
description: Test whether the SAM flag indicates that each segment is properly aligned according to the aligner (`0x2`).

name: is_unmapped
description: Test whether the read itself is unmapped according to the SAM flag.

name: is_next_segment_unmapped
description: Test whether the next segment in the template is flagged as unmapped (`0x8`).

name: is_reverse_complemented
description: Test whether `SEQ` is stored reverse complemented (`0x10`); for mapped reads this corresponds to reverse-strand alignment.

name: is_next_segment_reverse_complemented
description: Test whether `SEQ` of the next segment in the template is stored reverse complemented (`0x20`).

name: is_first_segment
description: Test whether the read is marked as the first segment in the template.

name: is_last_segment
description: Test whether the read is marked as the last segment in the template.

name: is_secondary
description: Test whether the alignment is marked as secondary.

name: is_qc_fail
description: Test whether the read failed vendor or pipeline quality checks.

name: is_duplicate
description: Test whether the alignment is flagged as a duplicate.

name: is_supplementary
description: Test whether the alignment is marked as supplementary.
